shared_properties: Saw body keeps a 24x24 px footprint and cold-steel palette that stands out from the stone environment.
The rotation center remains fixed so collision cues do not wobble across frames.
differences: This atlas supplies an eight-frame clockwise spin loop tuned for the late-game hazard density spike.

reference_images.label: Slip Down GDD mirror
reference_images.purpose: Saw size, 360-degree rotation rate, and damage role grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Cold metal finish and dark-room contrast grounding.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use TextureAtlasLayout and advance one frame every FixedUpdate interval that matches a 24 FPS visual spin loop.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load obstacles/saw_blade_rotor_sheet.png through AssetServer and bind the eight-frame clip to a hazard visual component keyed by asset_key.
runtime_notes: Keep the center axle pixel fixed in all frames.
Do not exceed the 24x24 bounds with tooth tips.

context.role: Rotating late-stage hazard that raises pressure in dark and abyss sections.
context.usage: Appears in the dark zone and abyss zone where precise friction braking is required.

deliverables.notes: Eight 24x24 frames for the rotor spin loop.
Single spin_loop clip definition for the saw hazard.

acceptance_criteria: Sheet size is exactly 192x24 px with eight 24x24 frames.
Center axle pixel stays fixed across all frames so collision feedback feels stable.
Tooth highlights remain readable on dark backgrounds without using colors outside the declared palette.
